name: North Sinai Governorate (Egypt)

colors.symbolism: the field of the flag and the sands of northern Sinai
the date palms and oases of North Sinai, including those at El Arish
the desert and the heritage of Sinai
the rocky hills of North Sinai
details and inscriptions of the emblem

description: A white field charged in the centre with the multi-coloured emblem of North Sinai Governorate, featuring green palm-tree motifs and grey, yellow and black detailing.
history: The flag of North Sinai Governorate displays its emblem on a white field, adopted as part of the system of governorate flags introduced in the 2000s. North Sinai is a peninsular governorate stretching from the Suez Canal to Rafah on the Mediterranean coast.

features.symbolism: the emblem of North Sinai with motifs of date palms, the Mediterranean coast, and the Bedouin heritage of the peninsula
